DISCOVERY 610, TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS MODIFIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, SET TWO, AND FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND ITS ATTORNEY GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART: A. AS TO NOS. 9, 10, 15, 16, 17, 21, 27, DENIED, BASED ON AMENDED ANSWER ADMITTING LIABILITY B. AS TO NOS. 22-24 AND 26, INCLUSIVE, GRANTED; DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE A CODE COMPLIANT SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE WITHIN TEN DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. C. AS TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSION NO. 29 AND THE REMAINING, SPECIFIED REQUESTS PERTAINING TO DEFENDANT'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, MOTION DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO FURTHER REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS THAT ADDRESS THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ASSERTED IN DEFENDANT'S AMENDED ANSWER. D. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS GRANTED; SANCTIONS SHALL ISSUE IN THE SUM OF $290.00 AGAINST DEFENDANTS ONLY, PAYABLE WITHIN THIRTY DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion Adm Otion For Order For Sanctions And/Or To Compel A Further Response To Pltf'S Form Interrogatroriy No. 15.1 Against Defts And Its Attorney
Off Calendar Sep-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Gail M. McMahon, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: James Shotwell
Mark D. Lipton, Deputy City Attorney, for Defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Plaintiff's Motion for sanctions and/or to compel further response to plaintiff's form interrogatory no. 15.1 and for against defendant and its attorney granted in part:
a. Within ten days of notice of entry of this order, Defendant shall serve a verified supplemental response to form interrogatory no. 15.1 that makes specific reference to its affirmative defenses;
b. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $290.00 against defendants only, payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order.

Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Order Compelling Further Response Sot Pltf'S Request For Admission, Set Two, And Sanctions Against Deft City And County Of San Fracsico
Off Calendar Sep-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Gail M. McMahon, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: James Shotwell
Mark D. Lipton, Deputy City Attorney, for Defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Plaintiff's Motion for order compelling further responses to plaintiff's request for admissions, set two, and for sanctions against defendant and its attorney granted in part, denied in part:
a. As to Nos. 9, 10, 15, 16, 17, 21, 27, denied, based on amended answer admitting liability
b. As to Nos. 22-24 and 26, inclusive, granted; defendant shall serve a code compliant supplemental response within ten days of notice of entry of this order.
c. As to request for admission no. 29 and the remaining, specified requests pertaining to defendant's affirmative defenses, motion denied without prejudice to further request for admissions that address the affirmative defenses asserted in defendant's amended answer.
d. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $290.00 against defendants only, payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling A Response To Pltf'S Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 And Sanctions Against Deft City And County Of San Francisco And Its Attorney Mark D. Lipton;
Off Calendar Sep-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Gail M. McMahon, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: James Shotwell
Mark D. Lipton, Deputy City Attorney, for Defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Plaintiff James Shotwell's Motion for order compelling a response to plaintiff's form interrogatory no. 17.1 and for sanctions against defendant and its attorney granted. Within ten days of notice of entry of this order defendant shall serve a verified supplemental response to form interrogatory no. 17.1 that addresses its prospective responses, as order in the accompanying motion, to request for admissions 22-24, and 26. Request for Sanctions denied.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Aug-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JAMES SHOTWELL Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Gail McMahon, Esq. McMahon Law Offices (415) 979-0555 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Mark D. Lipton, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4218, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for judgment on the pleadings granted, thirty days leave to amend. Defendant must plead facts supporting its affirmative defenses. (CCP section 438(c)(1)(A) and 438(c)(2)(B); FPI Development, Inc. vs. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Pltf'S Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions Against Deft Ccsf, Declaration Of Gail M. Mcmahon, Memorandum Of P/A, And Separate Statement;

MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Gail M. McMahon, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: James A. Shotwell
Mark D. Lipton, Deputy City Attorney, for defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted.
1. Plaintiff James Shotwell's Motion for order compelling further response to plaintiff's form interrogatories and for sanctions against defendant granted in part, denied in part:
a. Defendant shall serve verified, supplemental responses to interrogatory nos. 15.1, 16.1, and 16.7;
b. Denied as to interrogatory nos. 12.1, 12.3, and 12.4;
c. Defendant shall provide a code compliant verification to all response to plaintiff's interrogatories;
d. Any responses ordered herein shall be served within seven days of the date of service of this order;
e. Request for sanctions denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

After hearing, tentative ruling is modified.
2. Plaintiff James Shotwell's Motion for order compelling further response to plaintiff's first inspection demand; and for sanctions against defendant:
a. Any responses ordered herein shall be served within seven days of the date of service of this order,
b. Defendant shall serve code compliant responses to plaintiff's inspection demand, including verification wich delete the phrase "subject to inadvertent and undiscovered errors"
c. Defendant shall produce or issue documents identified for production at the offices of the Public Untilities Commission within 30 days.
d. As to inspection demand no. 14, defendant shall provide a code compliant, duly verified, supplemental response;
e. Request for sanctions is Denied.
Plaintiff to prepare proposed order.
